title: Pioneer (vertical restraints)
text:
On 2 February 2017, the European Commission initiated formal antitrust proceedings 
against Pioneer Corporation, Pioneer Europe N.V. and all legal entities directly or 
indirectly controlled by them, in relation to suspected agreements that fix the retail 
price or impose a minimum sale price and/or impose territorial restrictions in relation 
to electrical products in the Economic European Area. 
 
The Commission suspects that Pioneer may have breached EU rules prohibiting anti-
competitive agreements (Article 101 of the Treaty on the Functioning of the European 
Union and Article 53 of the EEA Agreement). The initiation of proceedings is based 
on Article 2(1) of Commission Regulation No 773/2004. 
 
In particular, the Commission intends to investigate whether agreements between 
Pioneer and its retailers amounted to fixing the retail price or imposing a minimum 
sale price and/or territorial restrictions on Pioneer's retailers. 
 
The initiation of proceedings does not signify that the Commission has made a 
definitive finding of an infringement but merely signifies that the Commission will deal 
with the case as a matter of priority.
sectors: G.47.91 - Retail sale via mail order houses or via Internet
